This was needed to be able to write tests that pass in bytes. `read` takes in a list of file paths, but if you provide a single string it will wrap that in a list. But python 3 treats strings and bytes differently, and in the code specifically for `read` it doesn't take bytes into account (which is probably a bug). But since a `bytes` object is iterable, it just silently does the wrong thing.

This is needed so that tests can be written that pass in raw bytes and have them function on both python 2 and python 3.

If I understand the script correctly, bam files are required?

So I suggest to make them a positional argument (which is always required).
Another option would be to use the `required` keyword argument when calling `add_argument()`.

Are other arguments optional or also always required?

Another thing: I wouldn't be afraid to use really short option flags, e.g. `-g` for genome id and `-l` for locus.

Also, we typically follow the pattern to use double quotes for strings meant for human consumption and use single quotes for everything else. Since arguments and help messages are things that users of the tool will see, put them in double quotes.

To sum up, you could replace these 3 lines with the following:
```python
parser.add_argument("bam_files", nargs='+', metavar="BAM_FILE", help="List of paths to bam files")
parser.add_argument("-g", "--genomeid", help="Genome id")
parser.add_argument("-l", "--bam_locus", help="Locus where to open IGV")
```

if only bam files are required arguments which are treated as positonal arguments.

The tool's help would now look like:
```
usage: make_igv_session.py [-h] [-g GENOMEID] [-l BAM_LOCUS]
                           BAM_FILE [BAM_FILE ...]

Create igv session

positional arguments:
  BAM_FILE              List of paths to bam files

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -g GENOMEID, --genomeid GENOMEID
                        Genome id
  -l BAM_LOCUS, --bam_locus BAM_LOCUS
                        Locus where to open IGV
```

This is the line that should fix #39. Travis's [skip_cleanup](https://docs.travis-ci.com/user/deployment/#uploading-files-and-skip_cleanup) option:

>When deploying files to a provider, prevent Travis CI from resetting your working directory and deleting all changes made during the build ( git stash --all) by adding skip_cleanup to your .travis.yml:

Means that if Travis rewrote the shebangs, they wouldn't get reverted back. This will ensure that the deployed code matches our repository's code. This was originally added in 6133d53c782ae2577275e4575026fbc8dee52881 because we were using a markdown README. Now that we use RST, this is not needed.
